title: Build a wheel module
description: Assemble a single drive pod: drive motor, gearbox, and 4" traction wheel. Build four of these in total — two per side. Breakdown on next step.
alt: Close-up of a single drive motor, gearbox, and 4-inch traction wheel subassembly

title: Drive pod reference drawing
description: Use this drawing for hardware callouts and orientation detail while assembling each drive pod.
alt: Engineering drawing of the drive pod subassembly

title: Build a omni module
description: Assemble a omni pod: one per side, two total.
alt: Side view detail of a traction wheel, bearing block, and axle shaft

title: Mount three wheel modules into the side rail
description: Drop three completed wheel modules into one perforated side rail and fasten them through the rail. Repeat to produce a mirrored second side. See next slide for different angle.
alt: Three traction wheels installed in one perforated side rail, front-on view

title: Bolt the drive motors to the side rail
description: Bolt two drive motors to the inside face of the same side rail and couple them to their wheels. Repeat steps 3–4 for the opposite rail before joining them.
alt: One drive side rail with three wheels and two drive motors fitted

title: Join both side rails into the wheelbase
description: Connect the two completed side rails with the cross-tubes to form the six-wheel drivetrain. Check that the frame is square before tightening fasteners.
alt: Two drive side rails joined by cross-tubes forming the complete drivetrain

title: Mount the belly-pan / electronics plate
description: Drop the central electronics-mount plate onto the drivetrain and fasten it to the cross-tubes. This plate carries the battery, PDH, and motor controllers.
alt: Drivetrain with the central electronics-mount belly pan installed

title: Add front RHS mounts
description: Use T-Brackets on top and bottom to mount front supports
alt: Drivetrain with belly pan installed, alternate angle

title: Add side SHS bars
description: Use Bolts to attach SHS in corners
alt: Front-quarter view of the drivetrain with belly pan installed

title: Lay out the electronics on the belly pan
description: Top-down reference for placement of the PDH, breakers, motor controllers, roboRIO. Lay components out before drilling or fastening anything.
alt: Top-down view of the electronics layout on the belly pan

title: Reference: side-panel exploded view
description: Pay close attention to the layout of the four folded plates showing their orientation and fastener pattern. Use this as a reference if a panel is installed backwards.
alt: Exploded view of the four superstructure side plates

title: Install the side superstructure panels
description: Bolt the two side superstructure plates and intake ramp to the drive base. These panels carry the intake and dispenser hardware in later steps.
alt: Two side superstructure panels bolted to the drive base

title: Install Left-side Motor
description: The motor shaft should have a keyway to hex adapter, with a 50t gear and 24t pulley attached.
alt: Side panels and electronics, alternate angle B

title: Install Right-side Motor
description: The motor shaft should have a 10t pinon gear mounted, with a key in the keyway, and a retaining ring.
alt: Side panels and electronics, alternate angle C

title: Add Indexing Shaft
description: This shaft contains in order: Collar, 84t gear, bearing, long spacer, wheel, long spacer, wheel, short spacer, 4x flap wheel, short spacer, wheel, long spacer, wheel, long spacer, bearing, 53t pulley, collar.
alt: Side panels and electronics viewed from the battery bay side

title: Add the upper intake roller
description: Install the upper intake roller assembly between the side panels.
alt: Front intake rollers installed with a game piece staged for reference

title: Install the front intake shaft
description: Install the front intake roller assembly between the side panels.
alt: Upper intake and dispenser shaft installed above the front rollers

title: Assemble the Launcher Shaft
description: Ensure steel flywheels are embedded securely inside the wheels.
alt: Full intake and dispenser assembly on the drivetrain, upper guards removed

title: Fit the Hood Rails
description: Install the upper hood guards.
alt: Upper coral and note dispenser guards installed on the near-complete robot

title: Add Hopper Storage
description:
Zip-tie the final polycarbonate plates to the structure. Use velcro to secure the battery door. 
You now have a fully assembled robot. Walk around the robot and confirm every subsystem is fastened, wired, and clear of moving parts.
alt: Fully assembled WA Robotics 2026 kit robot, hero view
